steps.m: he frowned (followed by وَتَوَلَّىٰٓ - and turned away)
steps.n: Added fathas to show this happened in the past. This word is connected to the next word وَتَوَلَّىٰٓ (and turned away) to show two actions that happened together.

steps.m: to turn away from or take charge of

steps.n: The root letters were connected with vowels to make 'he turned away' in the past tense. The ي at the end becomes ى when it's at the end of a word.
Adding تَ shows the person is both doing and receiving the action - they turned themselves away

steps.m: and he turned himself away (after عَبَسَ/he frowned)
steps.n: Added و at start to connect it with the previous word عَبَسَ (he frowned)
Added elongation marks (ٰٓ) to extend the final sound for recitation

steps.m: that (introduces what follows: جَآءَهُ ٱلْأَعْمَىٰ - the blind man came to him)
steps.n: This is a common connecting word that introduces what happens next, like saying 'that' in English. Here it connects to tell us what happened - that the blind man came.
Basic past tense verb pattern with fatha marks

steps.m: he came to him (referring to the blind man in ٱلْأَعْمَىٰ)
steps.n: Added هُ at the end which means 'him'. This shows who was affected by the coming. In this verse, it refers to the blind man mentioned right after (ٱلْأَعْمَىٰ).
Added vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Changed from 'became blind' to 'blind' using pattern أَفْعَل to make it a descriptive word meaning 'more/most blind'

steps.m: the blind one (who came to the Prophet ﷺ)
steps.n: Added the definite article ال and the alif maksura (ى) at the end. This refers to Abdullah ibn Umm Maktum who came to learn from the Prophet ﷺ.

steps.m: what (asking about يُدْرِيكَ - what could make you know)
steps.n: This is a question word used to ask about something unknown. In this ayah, it's asking about what could make you know something.

steps.m: and what (asking about يُدْرِيكَ - what could make you know)
steps.n: The و connects this question to what came before it, like saying 'and' in English

steps.m: to know deeply or be fully aware

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the past tense verb
Added يُ at start to show present tense, and changed vowels to show causative meaning (making someone else know)

steps.m: what would make you know (addressing Prophet Muhammad ﷺ)
steps.n: Added كَ at the end to show that 'you' (Prophet Muhammad ﷺ) are the one being made to know. In this ayah, it's part of the phrase 'وَمَا يُدْرِيكَ' meaning 'and what would make you know?'
This is a particle that expresses hope or possibility
Added هُۥ at the end to show who the hope is about. This connects to the next word يَزَّكَّىٰٓ (purify himself) - together meaning 'perhaps he will purify himself'

steps.m: to purify, to grow in purity

steps.n: Added يَ at the beginning to show the action is happening in the present
Added shadda on first root letter to show intensity and reflexive meaning
The ى at the end shows this is a reflexive verb (doing action to oneself) with a weak final letter

steps.m: or (connecting يَذَّكَّرُ with what comes before)
steps.n: This is a basic conjunction particle that gives a choice between two options. In this ayah, it connects the possibility of someone remembering (يَذَّكَّرُ) with another possibility mentioned before.
This is the basic meaning of the root
The doubled middle letter shows doing the action more strongly or repeatedly
Added تَ to show doing the action to/for oneself
Changed to present tense with يَ and assimilated تَ with ذ creating ذّ. This word is the subject of فَتَنفَعَهُ (so it benefits him) in this ayah.
This is the basic meaning of the root
Basic past tense verb form with fatha vowels
Added تَ at start to make it 'she/it' in present tense, and ُ at end shows ongoing action

steps.m: and then it gives benefit (connected to يَذَّكَّرُ - he receives reminder)
steps.n: Added فَ at start to show this happens after يَذَّكَّرُ (he receives reminder). The َ at the end shows this is a goal or result

steps.m: and then it gives him benefit (referring to the person in يَذَّكَّرُ)
steps.n: Added هُ to show who receives the benefit - it refers back to the same person mentioned in يَذَّكَّرُ (the one who receives the reminder)
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make it a past tense verb
Changed from a verb (he remembered) to a noun (reminder) by adding ى at the end and changing the vowels inside
Added ٱل (the) at the beginning which causes the ذ to get a shadda. This word refers to the reminder that might benefit the person mentioned in the ayah.
This is the basic form of the word that shows contrast

steps.m: As for... (introduces a topic before مَنِ ٱسْتَغْنَىٰ)
steps.n: These are actually two different words - أَمْ means 'or' while أَمَّا means 'as for'. They're not related by transformation.
This is a word that points to people in general, like saying 'whoever' or 'the one who' in English. It's used to start describing someone based on what they do.
Added kasra (ِ) at the end to show this word connects to the verb after it (ٱسْتَغْنَىٰ meaning 'became self-sufficient'). It's like saying 'the one who became self-sufficient'.
Added vowels to connect the letters and show it's a past tense verb

steps.m: he acted like he didn't need anyone
steps.n: Added ٱسْتَ at the start to show he's acting like he's rich/self-sufficient, even if he's not really rich
Added the small alif (ٰ) to show this is the final form

steps.m: you (when speaking to one male person)
steps.n: This is a personal pronoun used to address a single male person directly

steps.m: and so you/then you (addressing the Prophet ﷺ about the rich man)
steps.n: The فَ prefix shows this is a result or consequence of what was mentioned before. In this ayah, it's explaining how the Prophet ﷺ was giving attention to the rich man.
This is a preposition that shows something belongs to or is for someone
Added هُۥ (him) to show who the action is directed towards. This word connects to تَصَدَّىٰ (you attend to) in the ayah, showing who is being attended to.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to create basic past tense verb
The shadda (doubled letter) makes the action more intense
The تَ at the start shows the person is doing the action to themselves - turning themselves intensely (towards something)
The ى is written with a superscript alif (ىٰ) when it occurs at the end of the word, following Arabic orthographic rules
This is a negative particle that makes the verb يَزَّكَّىٰ (purify himself) negative
The و connects this negative statement to what came before it
Form used for feminine superlative (meaning 'highest')
The ى changes to ي before adding attachments. This is just a spelling change that doesn't affect the meaning.
Added ك at the end to show this is happening to 'you'. In this ayah, Allah is addressing Prophet Muhammad ﷺ.
This is the basic word for saying 'no' or making something negative
Adding أَ and merging the letters with shadda (ّ) changes this from just 'not' to 'that not'. This word connects to يَزَّكَّىٰ (purifies himself) to show what is being questioned.
Added يَ at the beginning to show this happens in the present time
The doubled letters (ّ) show this is done with extra effort or intensity, and it's something he does to himself
The small alif (ٰ) on top of ى shows this is the end of the word and helps with pronunciation
This particle comes from a root meaning 'front/forward', which evolved to mean 'regarding' or 'as for' since it brings something to the front of attention
Adding ا at the end turns this into a particle that introduces a new topic or contrast

steps.m: and as for (introduces contrast with previous statement)
steps.n: Adding وَ (and) at the beginning connects this contrast with what came before
This is a relative pronoun that refers to the person who is coming eagerly (يَسْعَىٰ) to the Prophet. In this context, it refers to a poor person seeking guidance.
The root letters changed to make 'he came' by adding vowels and turning the middle letter ي into ا
Adding the long alif makes the 'coming' more emphatic or intense
Added كَ at the end to show the action was directed towards 'you'
Connected the root letters س-ع-ي and added vowels to make the past tense 'he' form. The ي changes to ى (alif maqsurah) at the end.
Added يَ at the beginning to show present tense, and changed the middle vowel to sukun (ْ) which is common in present tense verbs

steps.m: he moves quickly/strives (in this ayah, describing someone coming eagerly)
steps.n: Added the small alif (ٰ) above the ى to show it's a long vowel. This is just a spelling variation that doesn't change the meaning.
This is a personal pronoun meaning 'he'
Added و at the start to mean 'and'. This word is the subject (doer) of يَخْشَىٰ (fears) in this ayah.
This is the basic meaning of the root
Adding vowels turns the root into a complete past tense verb showing fear
Added يَ at the beginning to show present tense. The final ي changes to ى because it's at the end of a present tense verb.

steps.m: he is afraid (of what comes next in the ayah)
steps.n: Added small alif (ٰ) above the ى to show proper pronunciation

steps.m: you (when speaking to one male person)
steps.n: This is a personal pronoun used to address a single male person directly
The فَ prefix shows this is a result or consequence - in this ayah, it connects to the verb تَلَهَّىٰ (you turn away), showing 'and as a result, you turn away'
This is a preposition that shows distance or the topic being discussed

steps.m: away from him/about him (referring to the one who came seeking guidance)
steps.n: The هُ suffix shows who the preposition is directed to - in this case, it refers to the person seeking guidance mentioned earlier in the ayah. This connects to تَلَهَّىٰ (you turn away) which comes right after.
Added basic vowels to connect the root letters. The و changes to ا when vowels are added.
The ت at the start changes it to mean 'following' instead of just being distracted
The shadda (doubled letter) shows the action is stronger
The ى at the end is another way to show it's feminine past tense

steps.m: he deliberately busied himself (with عَنْهُ - away from it)
steps.n: Added the superscript alif (ىٰ) to show the correct pronunciation of the final sound
This is the basic form of the word used for strong negation
Adding the shadda (doubled letter) makes the meaning stronger

steps.m: No indeed! (strongly rejecting what came before)
steps.n: Adding the long alif at the end makes this a special word that strongly rejects or corrects what was said before. In this ayah, it introduces a new topic that contrasts with previous ideas.
This is a particle that makes what comes after it more emphatic. It connects to تَذْكِرَةٌ (reminder) to emphasize it.
The ها at the end refers back to تَذْكِرَةٌ (reminder), which is feminine in Arabic
This is the basic meaning that all words from this root are built from
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
Adds ت at start and changes vowels to make a noun meaning 'a reminder'
Added tanween (ٌ) to show this is talking about any reminder, not a specific one
This is a word that means 'whoever' and is often used to start conditional statements like 'whoever does X will get Y'
The فَ shows this is connected to what happens next in the ayah - 'whoever wills/wants'
The root letters show the basic concept of wanting or wishing
Added fatha marks and connected the letters. The middle weak letter ي changes to ا. This word is connected to ذَكَرَهُ (he remembered it) in the ayah.
Added آ to show this is a hollow verb (has a weak middle letter). This is just a spelling variation of شَاءَ.
The root letters show the basic concept of remembering or mentioning
Added vowel marks to show this happened in the past and was done by 'he'

steps.m: he remembered/mentioned him (acted on by شَآءَ)
steps.n: Added هُ at the end to show who is being remembered/mentioned. This word is being acted on by شَآءَ (he willed) in the ayah.
Basic preposition showing location or containment. In this ayah, it connects to صُحُفٍ (pages/scrolls) to show where something is located.
Added kasra (ِ) under ف to show how to pronounce it correctly
Changed spelling of final ي to ى which is common in Quranic writing. This word connects to صُحُفٍ (pages/scrolls) to show where something is located.

steps.m: to write or record on a surface
steps.n: The root letters form the base meaning related to writing or recording
Added damma vowels to create plural noun for written pages

steps.m: of written pages/scrolls (possessed by مُّكَرَّمَةٍ - honored/noble)
steps.n: Added tanween kasra to show this word is possessed by or describes something else (in this case, these are honored/noble written pages)
The root letters form the base meaning of nobility or honor
Added vowels and doubled middle letter to show intense honoring
Added مُ at start to change from 'doing the honoring' to 'receiving the honor' - like changing from 'he honored' to 'honored one'
Added ة to make it refer to a female who is honored
Added ٍ at end and shadda on م to show this describes the honored pages (صُحُفٍ) mentioned before it
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past-tense verb
Changed to show what was lifted rather than who did the lifting
Shows this is describing a feminine noun

steps.m: lifted/raised (describing a feminine thing that belongs to something)
steps.n: Added ٍ to show this thing belongs to or is connected to something else

steps.m: lifted/raised (describing a feminine thing that belongs to something)
steps.n: Added shadda for pronunciation because this word follows another word ending in a similar sound
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create basic past tense verb
Adding shadda makes the action more intense or thorough
Adding مُ at start shows this describes something that received the action of thorough purification
Adding ة makes this describe a feminine thing that is purified
Adding ٍ shows this word is possessed by or connected to something else, and the ّ on م shows emphasis
Connected the root letters and added fatha to make the basic singular noun form
Added أَ at start and ي in middle to make it plural
Added kasra at end to complete the plural form
Added بِ to show these are the hands that do the action. This word relates to سَفَرَةٍ (scribes/angels) showing whose hands are being referred to.
Changes from basic root to plural noun meaning 'those who write/record'
Adds genitive case ending to show possession or connection

steps.m: of noble scribes (in بِأَيْدِى سَفَرَةٍ - by the hands of noble scribes)
steps.n: Shows these are special angels who write/record, connected to 'hands' in the phrase 'by the hands of noble scribes'
Added vowels (fatha and damma) to make 'he was' past tense verb meaning noble/generous
Changes verb into adjective showing permanent quality
Changes from singular to plural by switching the middle vowels (i-a becomes i-aa)
Adds tanween to show this is describing the word بَرَرَةٍ (righteous ones) that follows it
Added short vowels (fatha) to make it a past tense verb meaning 'he was good'
Adding ة changes it from a verb to a plural noun meaning 'righteous people'

steps.m: righteous ones (matching the grammar of كِرَامٍۭ - noble ones)
steps.n: Added tanween kasra (ٍ) to match the grammar of كِرَامٍۭ that it's describing
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make it a past tense verb

steps.m: may he be destroyed! (referring to الْإِنسٰنُ - mankind)
steps.n: Changed vowels to make it passive, expressing a curse or strong disapproval. This is connected to the next word الْإِنسٰنُ (mankind) in the ayah.
Forms the noun 'human' - humans are called this because they are naturally social beings who seek familiarity with others
The ْ (sukun) is removed for easier pronunciation when connecting letters

steps.m: the human being (subject of قُتِلَ - was destroyed/cursed)
steps.n: Added 'the' at the beginning and ُ at the end to show this is the subject of قُتِلَ (was destroyed/cursed)
This is the basic form of the word ما which can mean different things based on context
Added fatha mark to show pronunciation

steps.m: how (used to express amazement at أَكْفَرَهُۥ)
steps.n: The alif is elongated with a special mark (آ) to show emphasis. In this ayah, it expresses amazement at how ungrateful humans can be, connecting to the word أَكْفَرَهُۥ (how ungrateful he is)
Added vowels to make past tense 'he' form
Form IV pattern shows causing someone to do the action
Added pronoun هُ to show who is being made to disbelieve
Word is already in Form IV causative pattern with pronoun suffix

steps.m: from (connects to خَلَقَهُۥ - He created him)
steps.n: This is a basic preposition that shows where something comes from or its origin. In this ayah, it's asking 'from what thing did He create him?'
This is a question word used to ask about qualities or types
Adding the fatha (َ) mark makes the word readable and gives it proper pronunciation

steps.m: from what kind (of شَىْءٍ did He create it)
steps.n: Added kasra at the end because it comes after the word مِنْ (from). This shows it's being affected by the word before it.
Forms the noun meaning 'thing'
Added vowels to make it a noun meaning 'thing'
Added tanween kasra at the end to show this word is indefinite (any thing) and is possessed by the word أَيِّ (which) that comes before it
Added fathas after each letter to make the past tense verb meaning 'he created'

steps.m: he created him (referring to man in أَىِّ شَىْءٍ)
steps.n: Added هُۥ at the end to show who was created. This connects to the question 'from what thing?' (مِنْ أَىِّ شَىْءٍ) in the ayah, referring to the creation of man.
This is a basic preposition showing what something is made from or comes from. In this ayah, it connects to نُّطْفَةٍ (a drop of fluid) to show what humans were created from.

steps.m: to drip or flow in tiny drops
steps.n: The root describes the basic action of dripping or flowing in small amounts

steps.m: a tiny drop (of special fluid that creates babies)
steps.n: Changed from action to thing by adding vowels and ة at the end. In the Quran, this word specifically means the special drop that Allah uses to create humans.

steps.m: a tiny drop (of special fluid that creates babies) (after مِن)
steps.n: The shadda (double letter mark) appears because this word comes after the word مِن (from/of) in the ayah

steps.m: from a tiny drop (of special fluid that creates babies)
steps.n: The ٍ at the end shows this word is connected to مِن (from/of) before it, like saying 'from a drop'
Added fatha marks to connect the letters and show this is a past tense verb
Added هُۥ at the end to show who was created. This word connects to فَقَدَّرَهُۥ (then He proportioned him) in the ayah, showing what happened next to the same 'him'.
The root means to measure or determine something's proper amount
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make it a past tense verb
Doubling the د shows this was done with extra precision and detail
فَ shows this happened after or as a result of what came before
Adds هُۥ at the end to show what was measured
This is the basic form of the connecting word that shows one thing happens after another

steps.m: then, next, afterwards (connects to السَّبِيلَ يَسَّرَهُۥ)
steps.n: Added damma (ُ) on first letter, fatha (َ) on second letter, and shadda (ّ) to strengthen the meaning. This word connects to what comes next in the ayah (السَّبِيلَ يَسَّرَهُۥ - the way, He made it easy), showing these events happened in sequence.
Changes from basic root to noun meaning a physical or spiritual path
Added ٱل (al) at the beginning to make it specific and doubled the س with shadda
Added fatha at the end to show this is the object that is being made easy in يَسَّرَهُۥ (He made it easy)
The ي at the start shows it's happening now
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make a basic verb
The shadda (doubled س) shows doing the action to something else - making something easy rather than just being easy

steps.m: he made it easy (referring to السَّبِيلَ/the path)
steps.n: Added هُ at the end to show what is being made easy - referring back to السَّبِيلَ (the path) mentioned earlier in the ayah
Starting with the basic unvoweled form of this connecting word

steps.m: then (connects to أَمَاتَهُۥ - He caused him to die)
steps.n: Added vowels and shadda to complete the word. This word connects two events in sequence, showing that death came after the previous event.
Added vowels to connect the root letters together
Adding أَ at the start makes it mean 'cause someone to do something'. The middle و changes to ا because of Arabic sound rules.

steps.m: he caused him to die (followed by فَأَقْبَرَهُۥ - then he buried him)
steps.n: The هُ suffix shows who the action was done to. The small و (ۥ) is just for pronunciation.
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic past tense verb
Adding أَ at the beginning makes it causative - 'to make something be buried'

steps.m: and then he caused to be buried (after أَمَاتَهُۥ/he caused him to die)
steps.n: The فَ shows this happened right after He caused death, connecting it to the previous word أَمَاتَهُۥ

steps.m: and then He caused him to be buried
steps.n: The هُ shows who received the action (him). The ۥ is just a pronunciation mark.
This is the basic form of the connecting word that shows one thing happens after another

steps.m: then, next, afterwards (connects to إِذَا شَآءَ - when He wills)
steps.n: Added damma (ُ) and fatha (َ) marks to show how to pronounce it, and shadda (ّ) to make the م sound stronger. This word connects to what happens next in the ayah (إِذَا شَآءَ - when He wills).
This is the basic form of the word that means a specific point in time
Adding ا at the end changes it from a specific time to a conditional time. In this ayah, it introduces when Allah wills (شَآءَ).
Added vowel marks (fatha) to make it a past tense verb meaning 'he wanted'
The weak letter changes cause the hamza to move to the end

steps.m: he spread out/brought back to life
steps.n: Added vowel marks to connect the letters and show this happened in the past

steps.m: he made (something) come back to life
steps.n: Adding أَ at the start makes it mean 'to cause/make something happen'

steps.m: he made him/it come back to life (after شَآءَ - he willed)
steps.n: Added هُ at the end to show who receives the action. This happens after Allah wills it in the ayah.
This is the basic form of the word that expresses prevention or stopping
Adding the shadda makes the meaning stronger

steps.m: No! By no means! (used for strong denial or rejection)
steps.n: Adding alif at the end turns it into an emphatic particle that strongly rejects what came before
This is an emphatic particle that adds certainty to what follows
Adding مَّ (with shadda) changes the meaning to indicate something hasn't happened yet

steps.m: not yet (indicates something hasn't happened up to now)
steps.n: Adding ا at the end completes the particle. In this ayah, it connects with يَقْضِ (fulfill) to show that something hasn't been fulfilled yet
The root letters show the basic meaning of judging or deciding
Added vowel marks to the root letters to make it mean 'he decided/completed' in the past
Changes from past to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing middle vowel to kasra

steps.m: he decides/completes (after لَمَّا which requires dropping the final ي)
steps.n: The final ي is dropped after لَمَّا (meaning 'not yet') - this is like saying 'he has not yet decided'
This is the basic form of the negative particle 'ma' that negates the meaning of what comes after it
Added fatha mark to show correct pronunciation
Extended the alif with a maddah to show long vowel sound. This is just a spelling variation and doesn't change the meaning.
Added fathas to connect the letters and show this is a past tense verb

steps.m: he commanded him (object of يَقْضِ - he fulfills)
steps.n: Added هُ at the end to show who received the command. The ۥ is just a pronunciation helper. This word is affected by the verb يَقْضِ (he fulfills/completes) in this ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense form
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing middle and end vowels
Adds لِ to make it a command/suggestion 'let him...'
Added فَ (then/so) at start and changed final vowel to kasra because it's followed by another word (ٱلْإِنسٰنُ)
Added kasra (ِ) at end because it connects to next word
Changes the root into a noun for the species of humans, highlighting their social nature
Transforms the word from general mankind to a single human being
Adds ٱلْ to make it specific

steps.m: the human being (subject of فَلْيَنظُرِ - let him look)
steps.n: Adds ُ to show this is the subject doing the looking in the phrase 'let the human look'
Basic form of the preposition showing direction or destination
Added alif maksura (ىٰ) at the end. This is just a spelling variation that doesn't change the meaning. In this ayah, it connects 'look at' (يَنظُرِ) with 'food' (طَعَامِ), showing what to look at.
Added letters to change from the action of eating to the thing that is eaten
Added kasra (ِ) at the end because this word comes after the word إِلَىٰ (to)
Added هِۦٓ at the end to show the food belongs to him
Added emphasis by doubling the ن with a shadda

steps.m: that we/us (subject of صَبَبْنَا - we poured)
steps.n: Added نا at the end in the form of ا to show this is about 'we/us'. This word connects to the verb صَبَبْنَا (we poured) that comes right after it.
Connected the root letters and added vowel marks to make it past tense
Added 'نا' at the end to mean 'we' did the pouring

The letter ي (yaa) is a weak letter and often changes in different word forms. Here, it disappears, and the 'a' sound is added to the end.  The 'ء' (hamza) is also a weak letter and is often represented differently depending on the word's position.
The root letters change to make it easier to say - the middle and last letters become different letters (alif and hamza) to make the word 'water'
Added ٱلْ at the beginning to make it specific. The alif gets a special mark (آ) when followed by hamza.

steps.m: the water (object of صَبَبْنَا - we poured)
steps.n: Added fatha (َ) at the end because this word is receiving the action of pouring (صَبَبْنَا - we poured)
This is the basic concept of pouring something
The doubled middle letter shows strong or intense pouring
This word is what is being poured in the phrase 'We poured the water'. The ًا ending shows it's receiving the action.
This is a connecting word that links events in order. The shadda (doubled م) makes the word stronger.

steps.m: then (with emphasis, connecting to شَقَقْنَا - we split)
steps.n: Added damma (ُ) and shadda (ّ) to make the word stronger. This word connects to the next action in the verse (شَقَقْنَا - we split), showing what happened next.
Connected the root letters and added vowel marks to make it a past tense verb
Added نا at the end to show 'we' did the action
Connected the root letters and added basic vowels to make it a noun referring to the earth or ground
Adding ٱلْ makes it refer to the specific earth rather than any earth (this earth is being split in the ayah)
Adding fatHa shows this is the object being split in the phrase 'we split the earth'
The doubled letter shows intensity of the hardship
Connected the letters and doubled the middle letter to show intensity

steps.m: a split/crack (object of شَقَقْنَا - we split)
steps.n: Changed from a verb (action) to a noun (thing) by adding tanween, showing what got split
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make it a past tense verb
Adding أ at the start makes the verb causative - instead of something growing by itself, someone is making it grow
ف means 'and then' or 'so' - it shows this happened after something else

steps.m: and then We made (something) grow (referring to what follows in فِيهَا حَبًّا - in it grain)
steps.n: The نا shows that Allah is speaking as 'We'. This word connects to what follows (فِيهَا حَبًّا) which tells us what was made to grow - grain in it.
Added kasra (ِ) under ف to show how to pronounce it correctly

steps.m: in it (referring to the earth mentioned earlier)
steps.n: Added ها at the end to show we're talking about something specific that was mentioned before (the earth). The fatha (َ) on ه shows how to pronounce it correctly.
Basic three letter root meaning 'seed' - no transformation needed
Double letter in middle (ب becomes بّ) makes the word stronger/more intense, like saying 'a proper grain'

steps.m: a grain/seed (object of أَنۢبَتْنَا - we caused to grow)

steps.n: Basic noun pattern with kasra-fatha vowels
Added conjunction 'wa' at the beginning to mean 'and'
Added tanween fatah and alif to show this is the object of the verb أَنْبَتْنَا (We caused to grow) in this ayah

steps.m: vegetables (specifically fodder plants that are cut/harvested)
steps.n: The root meaning of cutting/harvesting evolved to specifically mean fodder vegetables that are harvested for animals
Added و (and) to connect this word with عِنَبًا (grapes) that came before it
Added tanween fatah to show indefinite accusative case, coordinated with عِنَبًا
Connected the root letters and added vowel marks to make it a noun
Added ون to make it refer to olives (the fruit) rather than oil
Added ًا to show this is an object in the sentence

steps.m: and an olive (as object of implied verb, connected to وَنَخْلًا)
steps.n: Added و to connect this with the next word وَنَخْلًا (and palm trees)
Added vowel marks to connect the letters and make them pronounceable
The و at the start means 'and'

steps.m: and a palm tree (object of verb)
steps.n: The ًا (tanween) at the end shows this is indefinite (a palm tree) and is the object being acted upon in the sentence
Connected the root letters with fatha to make it a past-tense verb

steps.m: a garden (because it's surrounded by walls)
steps.n: Added ي and ة to transform the verb into a noun for an enclosed space
Changes from singular 'garden' to plural 'gardens' by adding ا and changing internal vowels

steps.m: and gardens (connected to what came before)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to mean 'and', and َ at the end because it's being acted upon in the sentence
Changes to plural form describing multiple thick/dense things
Added tanween alif to show this word is indefinite and matches the case of حَدَآئِقَ (gardens) which it describes
Added alif after first letter and ة at end to create a noun for something that brings enjoyment
Added و with fatha at beginning to mean 'and'
Added tanween at end to show this is a direct object, and alif changed to alif with line above for writing style
Doubling the middle letter turns it from a verb into a noun meaning the grass/plants themselves
Adding tanween alif shows this is the object being mentioned in the list of Allah's provisions
Adding و connects this to the previous word فٰكِهَةً (fruit) in the list of provisions
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make it a past tense verb
Added alif after second root letter to make it a noun meaning the thing that provides benefit
Adding shadda (doubling mark) on م makes the meaning stronger
Adding ً (tanween) makes it indefinite and shows it's being used as an object
Added a special mark (tanween alif) at the end to show this is describing 'how' something was given - like saying 'as a provision'
Added fatha to show basic vowel sound
Added shadda to emphasize the meaning, making it stronger

steps.m: surely for you all (connected to مَّتٰعًا - enjoyment)
steps.n: Added كُمْ to show this is directed at multiple people. This word connects to مَّتٰعًا (enjoyment) to show who the enjoyment is for.
The plural form أنعام specifically refers to grazing livestock (like camels, cattle, sheep) which were seen as a major blessing and source of sustenance
The kasra (ِ) shows this word is connected to something else in the sentence
Added preposition لِ meaning 'for/to'
Added و which connects this phrase to what came before it (مَّتٰعًا لَّكُمْ - enjoyment for you)
Added second person masculine plural possessive pronoun suffix كُمْ meaning 'your'
This is a time word that introduces something that will happen. In this ayah, it introduces when the deafening blast comes.

steps.m: and/then when (followed by جَآءَتِ - it comes)
steps.n: The فَ shows this happens after something else mentioned before. It connects this event (the deafening blast coming) with what was discussed earlier.
The middle weak letter alif replaces the original ya
Added تِ to show the subject is feminine. This word is describing what الصَّآخَّةُ (the deafening blast) does.

steps.m: to make a loud, deafening sound

steps.n: Added vowels and doubled the middle letter to show strong action
Added alif after first letter and ة at end to make it a noun describing something that makes this sound

steps.m: The Deafening Blast (a name for the Day of Judgment)
steps.n: Added ٱل to make it definite and ُ to show it's the subject. The آ (long alif) is used instead of ا for emphasis. This is one of the names for the Day of Judgment because it will begin with a massive, deafening blast.
Connected the letters together with vowel marks to make it a noun. The middle letter و keeps its sound because it has a sukoon mark.
Added a fatha mark at the end to show this word is in the accusative case (used as an object)
When two same letters come together, they combine with a shadda

steps.m: he flees/is fleeing (from أَخِيهِ - his brother)
steps.n: Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start, changing middle vowel to kasra, and adding damma at end. In this ayah, it shows who the person is fleeing from.
Basic noun pattern showing one person
Adding the definite article ال makes it definite
The little mark above shows this person is doing the action
This is a basic preposition that shows separation or moving away from something. In this ayah, it shows someone fleeing from their brother.
Connected the root letters and added fatha to make the basic word for 'brother'
Added kasra at the end to show this word is being used as an object of a preposition
Added ي at the end to show possession - 'my brother'

steps.m: his brother (possessed by the person being fled from)
steps.n: Added هِ to show it belongs to him. This word is connected to مِنْ (from) in the phrase 'flees from his brother'

steps.m: to be a mother, to lead as a mother does

steps.n: The ُ (damma) sound makes this into the noun 'mother'
Added coordinating conjunction wa- to connect with previous phrase
The vowel at the end changes to show this mother belongs to someone
Added 'his' to show possession. This word pairs with 'and his father' (وَأَبِيهِ) to show both parents.

steps.m: to care for as a father

steps.n: The root letters أ-ب-و form the basic word أَب meaning 'father'
Adding ي at the end makes it mean 'my father' instead of just 'father'
Adding هِ to show possession - 'his father'
Adding و at the beginning to mean 'and'

steps.m: to be a companion to/accompany closely

steps.n: Added alif after first letter to create a noun meaning 'one who accompanies'
Added ة to make the word refer to a female companion
Adding conjunction 'and' at beginning of word
The ة at the end changed to ت when connecting to the next word
Adding هِ (hi) at the end means 'his companion' instead of just 'companion'. The و (wa) at the start means 'and'
Changed the vowels to make it a plural noun meaning 'sons'
Adding هِ means 'his'. This word is connected to صٰحِبَتِ (his companion/wife) through the conjunction وَ (and)
Adding وَ at the start means 'and', connecting this word to the previous word صٰحِبَتِ (his companion/wife)
The doubled ل shows this means 'every single one' with no exceptions. This word is connected to يُغْنِيهِ (will occupy him) in the ayah.
Adding لِ shows this means 'for' or 'to', and the kasra (ِ) at the end shows it's affected by the لِ
Added sukun and kasra to form the noun pattern for 'person'. This word is what يُغْنِيهِ (it will occupy him) is talking about in this ayah.
Added ٱ (hamzat al-wasl) at start to help pronounce the word since it begins with two consonants
Added tanwin kasratayn (ٍ) at the end to show this means 'any person' rather than a specific person
This is a basic preposition that shows something comes from somewhere or belongs to something
Added a stronger sound (shadda) on م because it comes after ل in لِكُلِّ

steps.m: from them (referring to each person mentioned in لِكُلِّ ٱمْرِئٍ)
steps.n: Added هُمْ at the end to show this applies to multiple people. This connects back to 'every person' (لِكُلِّ ٱمْرِئٍ) mentioned earlier
Connected the letters together and added vowel marks to make the basic word for 'day'

steps.m: that day (when each person will be preoccupied with their own state)
steps.n: Added ئِذٍ which means 'that' or 'then', making it refer to a specific day being discussed. This word relates to the context of the Day of Judgment when everyone will be concerned with their own situation.

steps.m: to be concerned with something, to occupy oneself

steps.n: Added vowel marks to turn the action into a noun meaning 'a matter/affair'. This word is what preoccupies/concerns someone in the verse.
Added double damma to show this is the main subject doing the action in the sentence
Added vowel marks to connect the root letters and show this is a past tense verb
Changes from past to present tense and adds 'makes/causes' meaning
Adding هِ suffix meaning 'him' as the object of the verb. The word شَأْنٌ (matter/affair) is doing the preoccupying.
Connected the root letters together to form the basic singular noun for 'face'
Changed vowels to damma and added و to make it plural, showing there are many faces

steps.m: faces (as the subject of مُّسْفِرَةٌ - bright/beaming)
steps.n: Added double damma to show this word is the subject being described as bright/beaming in this verse
Added fatha at the end to connect it with the next part

steps.m: on that day (faces on that day are bright)
steps.n: Added ئِذٍ to specify 'that day' mentioned earlier. This word connects to وُجُوهٌ (faces) in this ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha to make basic past tense verb
The مُ prefix and kasra make this describe who/what does the action (like adding -ing/-er in English)
The ة makes this word feminine to match وُجُوهٌ (faces) which is feminine in Arabic
The shadda on م makes the meaning stronger - these faces aren't just shining, they're brightly shining
The tanween (double damma) shows this describes faces in general, not specific faces
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the past tense verb
Changed to active participle form to show who does the action
Added feminine marker to match مُّسْتَبْشِرَةٌ which it describes

steps.m: a female who laughs happily (describes مُّسْتَبْشِرَةٌ)
steps.n: Added tanween to show this is indefinite, matching مُّسْتَبْشِرَةٌ which it describes
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
Added مست at the beginning to show someone is actively seeking and receiving good news (like saying 'self-receiving good news')

steps.m: female seeking and receiving good news
steps.n: Added ة to make it feminine

steps.m: a female who is joyfully receiving good news (paired with ضَاحِكَةٌ - laughing)
steps.n: Added shadda on م for emphasis, sukun marks for clear pronunciation, and tanween to show it's indefinite. This word pairs with ضَاحِكَةٌ (laughing) to describe faces that are both laughing and joyfully receiving good news.
Connected the root letters together to form the basic noun 'face'
Changes the vowel pattern to make the word plural, meaning more than one face
Adds tanween damma to show this word is doing something in the sentence

steps.m: and faces (that will have dust on them)
steps.n: Adds و with fatha at the beginning to connect this word to what came before it. These faces will have dust on them according to the next words (عَلَيْهَا غَبَرَةٌ)
Connected the root letters with vowel marks to form the basic noun
Added fatha at the end to show this word is being acted upon by something

steps.m: on that day (when faces will be covered with dust)
steps.n: Added ئِذٍ to specify 'that particular day' being discussed in the ayah. This word connects to what follows in the ayah about faces being covered with dust.
This is a preposition showing something is on top of or over something else
The ى changes to يْ before adding pronouns
Added ها to show this refers to the faces (وُجُوهٌ) mentioned earlier in the verse
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make a basic past-tense verb
Adding ة changes it from an action to a noun meaning one instance of dust
Adding tanween makes it 'a dust' instead of 'the dust'
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Added damma at end to show it's in the present tense nominative case

steps.m: she covers/overwhelms them (referring to the faces mentioned earlier)
steps.n: Added pronoun هَا to show who is being overwhelmed (referring back to the faces mentioned earlier in the verse)
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to create the basic noun form
Added ة to make it a single instance noun, and ٌ to show it's indefinite. This word describes what will cover the faces mentioned in the ayah.
This is the basic form of the word that means 'those'
The alif is extended to show emphasis
Added small alif above for additional emphasis

steps.m: those (pointing to people far away) (subject of هُمُ)
steps.n: Added ئِكَ at the end to show we're pointing to something far away. This word is the subject of هُمُ (they are) in this ayah.
This is the basic form of the pronoun that means 'they' or 'them' when referring to a group of males or a mixed group
Added damma marks to show this is the subject form of 'they'. In this ayah, it points forward to 'the disbelievers, the wicked ones' (ٱلْكَفَرَةُ ٱلْفَجَرَةُ)

steps.m: to reject/deny truth, to cover up, to be ungrateful

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
Adding ة at the end turns the verb into a plural noun meaning 'those who reject'
ٱلْ makes it specific ("the"), while ُ shows this word is doing something in the sentence

steps.m: to break out, burst forth wickedly

steps.n: Added vowels to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
Adding ة changes it from an action to describe people who do that action repeatedly
Added ٱلْ to make it specific and ُ to show it's the subject of هُمُ (they are) in this ayah
